title: campanula glomerata subsp. farinosa
intro: Campanula glomerata subsp. farinosa, also known as the clustered bellflower or pale bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, with its roots and leaves containing flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Campanula glomerata subsp. farinosa has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to leverage its medicinal properties and provide potential health benefits.
